concept.title: Section-Aware Chunking and Embedding Strategy
concept.shortdesc: How DITA's section structure enables semantically coherent chunks that preserve metadata context, and how those chunks are transformed into high-dimensional vectors for similarity search.

section.title: Why does naive text splitting fail?
section.p: Most RAG systems split documents by character count — every 1000 characters, start a new chunk. This approach has critical problems:

ul.li: Broken context: A chunk boundary splits a code example in half, making both halves meaningless
Mixed topics: A chunk contains the end of one section and the beginning of another, conflating two unrelated subjects
Lost identity: Plain text chunks have no metadata — the system cannot tell which chunk is about installation vs. troubleshooting
No hierarchy: All chunks appear equal, with no way to prioritize or filter

section.p: DITA solves all four problems because the XML structure explicitly marks section boundaries, topic types, and metadata.

section.title: How does section-aware chunking work?
section.p: Section-aware chunking uses DITA's XML structure as the chunk boundary instead of arbitrary character counts:
section.codeblock: DITA Topic: "Content Reuse Strategies" ├── shortdesc (inherited by all chunks) ├── prolog metadata (inherited by all chunks) ├── section[@id="why-reuse"] │ └── CHUNK 1: title + paragraphs (380 tokens) ├── section[@id="conref-mechanism"] │ └── CHUNK 2: title + paragraphs + codeblock (920 tokens) ├── section[@id="keyref-mechanism"] │ └── CHUNK 3: title + paragraphs + table (1100 tokens) └── section[@id="when-to-use-which"] └── CHUNK 4: title + paragraphs + table (780 tokens)
section.p: Each chunk is produced by one rule: one section = one chunk. The chatbot configuration specifies boundaries:

ul.li: Minimum: 100 tokens — sections smaller than this are merged with the next section
Maximum: 1500 tokens — sections larger than this are split at paragraph boundaries
Overlap: 2 sentences from the end of the previous chunk are prepended for continuity

section.title: How does metadata attach to each chunk?
section.p: Every chunk inherits the full prolog metadata from its parent topic. The chunking process creates a JSON payload for each chunk:
section.codeblock: { "chunk_id": "content-reuse__conref-mechanism", "topic_id": "content-reuse", "topic_type": "concept", "section_id": "conref-mechanism", "section_title": "How does conref work?", "shortdesc": "Techniques for reusing content ...", "text": "A conref (content reference) pulls ...", "metadata": { "audience": "technical-writer", "difficulty": "intermediate", "content-domain": "content-reuse", "content-intent": "explain", "chatbot-priority": "high", "service-line": "dita-engineering", "xml:lang": "en-US", "created": "2026-01-15" }, "token_count": 920 }
section.p: This metadata is stored alongside the vector in the database, enabling filtered search (e.g., "show me only beginner content about publishing").

section.title: How are chunks converted to vectors?
section.p: Each chunk's text is passed through an embedding model that produces a high-dimensional vector representation:

row.entry: Best performance on technical content retrieval benchmarks
Full dimensionality preserves fine-grained semantic distinctions between technical concepts
Scale-invariant; works well with normalized embeddings
Prepending the shortdesc gives each chunk topic-level context

section.p: The embedding input is constructed as: [shortdesc] [section_title]: [chunk_text]. This ensures the embedding captures both the topic-level context and the section-level detail.

section.title: Before and after: chunk quality comparison
section.codeblock:
Chunk: "...to install the toolkit. ## Setting Up You can configure the toolkit by editing the conf/configuration.properties file. The key settings are: - temp.dir: The temporary directory..."
Metadata: { source: "guide.xml", page: 12 } Embedding quality: LOW (mixed topics, no context)

Chunk: "How to configure the DITA Open Toolkit: You can configure the toolkit by editing the conf/configuration.properties file. The key settings are: temp.dir (the temporary directory)..."
Metadata: { topic_type: "task", audience: "build-engineer", difficulty: "intermediate", content-domain: "publishing", content-intent: "how-to", chatbot-priority: "high" } Embedding quality: HIGH (focused, typed, metadata-rich)
